title: Rottboellia robusta
taxonomy: Rottboellia robusta is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Rottboellia and is further classified as a member of the order Poales. Rottboellia robusta is a native species to the tropical and subtropical regions of Africa, Asia, and Australia. This species is also known as a robust Rottboellia or robust grass. It is a perennial grass that grows in a variety of habitats, including grasslands and savannas.

intro: Rottboellia robusta, also known as Rottboellia or Ku Gua, is a type of tropical grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Rottboellia robusta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Rottboellia robusta is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
